title: DeleteFaceFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing DeleteFaceFeature features in a component and supports the ability to create new DeleteFaceFeature features. The SurfaceDeleteFaceFeature and DeleteFaceFeature differ in that the SurfaceDeleteFaceFeature can delete any face without any restrictions. If the body is a solid, it will become a surface when the first face is deleted. The specified face is deleted without any other changes being made to the body. The DeleteFaceFeature deletes the specified face and also modifies the other faces in the body to heal or fill in the area of the deleted face. This means that a solid body will remain solid.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new SurfaceDeleteFace feature. This deletes the specified faces from their bodies and attempts to heal the body. The method will fail if the body cannot be healed. This is equivalent to selecting and deleting faces when in the Patch workspace.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified DeleteFaceFeature object using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified DeleteFaceFeature object using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of DeleteFaceFeature objects in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new SurfaceDeleteFace feature. This deletes the specified faces from their bodies and attempts to heal the body. The method will fail if the body cannot be healed. This is equivalent to selecting and deleting faces when in the Patch workspace.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created DeleteFaceFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: facesToDelete
parameters.description: A single BRepFace or an ObjectCollection containing multiple BRepFace objects.

classType.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified DeleteFaceFeature object using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified DeleteFaceFeature object using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of DeleteFaceFeature objects in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = deleteFaceFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = deleteFaceFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: DeleteFaceFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = deleteFaceFeatures_var.objectType
